description.en: Master Git branching strategies, pull requests, rebasing vs merging, and team collaboration workflows for professional Python development.
description.ta: Git branching strategies, pull requests, rebasing vs merging, மற்றும் தொழில்முறை Python development-க்கான team collaboration workflows கற்றுக்கொள்ளுங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Tree of Branches

Vaathiyaar draws a tree. "The trunk is your main branch. Every new feature is a branch growing from the trunk. When the feature is ready, you merge the branch back. If the branch goes bad, you prune it. The trunk stays healthy."

### Branching Strategies

**Feature Branch Workflow:**
1. Create a branch from main: `git checkout -b feature/login`
2. Make commits on your branch
3. Push and open a Pull Request
4. Get code review
5. Merge to main

### Branch Naming Conventions
- `feature/user-auth` — new feature
- `fix/login-crash` — bug fix
- `refactor/db-layer` — code cleanup
- `docs/api-guide` — documentation

### Merge vs Rebase

| | Merge | Rebase |
|---|-------|--------|
| History | Preserves all commits | Linear, clean history |
| Safety | Never rewrites history | Rewrites commit history |
| Use when | Merging to main | Updating feature branch |

### Pull Request Best Practices
- Keep PRs small (< 400 lines)
- Write clear descriptions
- Request specific reviewers
- Address all review comments
- Squash commits before merging

> **Key Concept:** Branches let you work on features in isolation. Pull requests let your team review code before it reaches main. This workflow prevents bugs and improves code quality.

explanation.en: Start from the main branch — always branch from main
Get the latest changes from the remote
Create and switch to a new feature branch
Work on your feature — edit files, add code
Stage specific files — not 'git add .' for safety

steps.line: git commit -m 'feat: add user profile page'

explanation.en: Conventional commit: 'feat:' prefix for new features
Push branch to remote and set upstream tracking
Create a PR for code review — describe what and why

steps.line: # After review, update your branch with main

explanation.en: If main has new commits, update your branch
Replay your commits on top of latest main — clean history
Safe force push — fails if someone else pushed to your branch

steps.line: # PR approved — squash and merge on GitHub

explanation.en: Squash all commits into one clean commit on main
Switch back to main and get the merged changes
Delete the feature branch locally — it's merged now

output: * a1b2c3d (HEAD -> main) feat: add user profile page (#42)

left.label: Merge (preserves history)
left.content:
* Merge branch 'feature/login'
|\
| * Add login form
| * Add auth logic
|/
* Previous commit

right.label: Rebase (linear history)
right.content:
* Add login form
* Add auth logic
* Previous commit
(clean, linear, no merge commit)

animation_sequence.title: Git Branch and Merge
animation_sequence.code:
# $ git checkout -b feature/login
# $ git add auth.py
# $ git commit -m 'Add login'
# $ git checkout main
# $ git merge feature/login
# $ git push origin main

executionSteps.explanation: Create and switch to feature branch

instruction.en: Write the sequence of Git commands to: 1) Create a feature branch from main, 2) Make a commit with conventional commit message, 3) Push with upstream, 4) After merge, delete the local branch.
instruction.ta: Git commands-ன் வரிசையை எழுதுங்கள்: 1) main-ல் இருந்து feature branch உருவாக்குங்கள், 2) conventional commit message-உடன் commit செய்யுங்கள், 3) upstream-உடன் push செய்யுங்கள், 4) merge-க்குப் பிறகு local branch-ஐ நீக்குங்கள்.

git checkout main
git pull origin main

practice_challenges.expected_output: Complete Git workflow from branch creation to cleanup

en: git checkout main && git pull && git branch -d feature/my-feature
ta: git checkout main && git pull && git branch -d feature/my-feature

en: Creates a new branch AND switches to it
ta: புதிய branch-ஐ உருவாக்கி அதற்கு switch செய்யும்

q.en: What is the advantage of rebase over merge?

en: It creates a clean, linear commit history

q.en: Why use --force-with-lease instead of --force when pushing?
q.ta: Push செய்யும்போது --force-க்கு பதிலாக --force-with-lease ஏன் பயன்படுத்த வேண்டும்?

en: It fails if someone else pushed to the branch, preventing data loss
ta: வேறொருவர் branch-க்கு push செய்திருந்தால் தோல்வியடையும், data loss-ஐ தடுக்கும்
